task_summary: Add explicit opt-in live macOS active-window probing to mew-ghost while preserving fixture-safe default rendering, dry-run launcher intents, structured fallbacks, README usage, and hermetic tests.
tests_run: UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state
UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state --live-active-window
UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state --output /tmp/mew-ghost-sp13-state.json
notes: SP13 was implemented by mew in session #26 with --model gpt-5.5 and write scope limited to experiments/mew-ghost. The first resumed run used the default local auth and failed with HTTP 401 token_expired before product edits; retrying with --auth /Users/mk/.codex/auth.json allowed the session to continue. Mew's first write batch #295-#297 reached the right source/test/docs shape but the focused verifier failed because one stale test assertion still expected mew-ghost.sp12.v1. Mew repaired in the same session with corrected batch #302-#304; auto-approval #305-#307 applied the corrected product files and the focused verifier passed 13 tests. Operator rejected stale approvals #295-#297 after the corrected batch was already applied. No Codex product-code rescue edits were made. A reusable M6.16 polish issue was opened as #10 because stale failed approvals remained pending and caused finish to record pending-approval state after the corrected verifier pass. The real live macOS Accessibility state was intentionally not asserted as a required pass condition; live behavior is opt-in and hermetically covered through injected providers/runners.
